page: Amazon Web Services r7i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it features 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor operates at a base clock speed of 3.2 GHz and is supported by a 105 MB L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the top tier and strong categories respectively. Text processing performance is particularly notable, reaching top-tier classification. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core metrics, aligns with average mid-range expectations. Memory bandwidth tests show 689.3 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and approximately 52.25 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. However, without GPU acceleration, the instance exhibits weak performance in medium-parameter LLM text generation tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r7i.2xlarge offers a dense memory profile relative to its compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without paying for excess CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the complete reliance on network-attached storage and the baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, which may limit high-throughput storage operations. This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive applications such as in-memory databases, caching layers like Redis, enterprise applications, and data processing tasks that fit within its 64 GB memory boundary.
description: The Amazon Web Services r7i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with a 3.2 GHz clock speed and 105 MB L3 cache, this instance utilizes the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It lacks local storage and GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps. Benchmark results show strong single-core performance, particularly in text processing and single-threaded compression, while multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics remain in the average tier. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core makes this instance qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads. It is highly suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity without excessive compute overhead.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7i.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high-speed DDR5 memory and dedicated Intel Xeon processors for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

Equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
No local storage or GPU, utilizing network-attached storage with 3 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications
